text: RAG stands for Retrieval-Augmented Generation, a method that improves text generation by retrieving external knowledge.
RAG combines a retriever and a generator to produce more accurate responses.
The retriever finds relevant documents from a knowledge base using similarity search.
The generator produces answers based on both the query and retrieved documents.
RAG reduces hallucinations by grounding responses in external data.
Without RAG, language models rely only on their internal knowledge stored in parameters.
NLP stands for Natural Language Processing, which enables computers to understand and generate human language.
Tokenization is a process in NLP that splits text into words or tokens.
Text preprocessing includes lowercasing, removing punctuation, and cleaning text.
Semantic search uses embeddings to find meaning-based similarity between texts.
Keyword matching is a simple retrieval method based on exact word overlap.
Large language models like GPT are trained on large datasets and generate text based on learned patterns.
Language models may produce hallucinations when they lack accurate information.
External knowledge sources help improve the reliability of generated answers.
Cloud computing enables scalable and flexible deployment of applications.
Load balancing distributes incoming requests across multiple servers to improve performance.
Microservices architecture divides applications into small, independent services.
APIs allow different systems to communicate with each other.
Databases are used to store and manage structured data efficiently.
MongoDB is a NoSQL database that stores data in document format.
JSON is a lightweight data format commonly used for data exchange.
Machine learning enables systems to learn patterns from data and make predictions.
Artificial intelligence includes techniques such as machine learning and natural language processing.
Large language models need RAG because their internal knowledge is limited and cannot be easily updated, so external information improves accuracy.
RAG is useful for knowledge-intensive tasks because it allows the model to retrieve relevant external information instead of relying only on parametric memory.
Natural Language Processing (NLP) is a branch of artificial intelligence that helps computers understand, interpret, and generate human language.
